jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car after the opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of pain/frustration
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated character moment but predictable

jokes.text: Homer, sitting that close to the TV can't be good for you. / Talking while the TV's on can't be good for you.
jokes.setup: Marge expresses concern about Homer sitting too close to TV
jokes.punchline: Homer deflects with equal but opposite concern about talking during TV
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Homer's self-serving logic and TV obsession

jokes.text: Nah. I wanna enjoy this show before the network retools it.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests Homer come to the mall instead of watching TV
jokes.punchline: Homer's cynical but accurate observation about TV network interference
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on television industry that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: How can I organize this modeling agency... and still be a good single father? - I love you, Daddy. - [Audience] Aww! [Groans] Too late!
jokes.setup: The Cyborganizer show gets retooled into a sappy family drama
jokes.punchline: Homer's disgusted reaction to the immediate network pandering
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the retooling setup, showing exactly what Homer feared

jokes.text: Just get me a deck of cards and I'll win whatever I need from the other kids. / But you need to try things on. Every brand has a different idea of 'husky.'
jokes.setup: Bart suggests gambling instead of shopping for camping supplies
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact reference to Bart's weight category in clothing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with unexpected dark turn about children's sizing

jokes.text: [Grunts] I'm in tantrum position. T-minus 5, 4, 3- Remembering dead cat for real tears, and-
jokes.setup: Bart doesn't want to go shopping and threatens a tantrum
jokes.punchline: He mechanically describes his tantrum process including manipulative fake crying
jokes.explanation: Excellent deconstruction of childhood tantrums with darkly funny methodology

jokes.text: Tantrum averted. But now I can't forget the cat. [Weeping]
jokes.setup: Bart successfully avoided shopping but used dead cat memory for tears
jokes.punchline: Now genuinely sad because he can't stop thinking about the dead cat
jokes.explanation: Great payoff showing consequences of Bart's manipulation backfiring emotionally

jokes.text: Now read the first line. / I... ate... pee-pee. [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Eye doctor testing Homer's vision with eye chart
jokes.punchline: Homer reads nonsense that sounds like bathroom humor and laughs
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor that fits Homer's character but relies on cheap bathroom comedy

jokes.text: That pair's popular with celebrities like Val Kilmer. / Ooh! My favorite Door. / And Yoko Ono. / Ew! She ruined the Plastic Ono Band.
jokes.setup: Optometrist mentions celebrity endorsements including Yoko Ono
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused criticism - she ruined her own band
jokes.explanation: Clever multi-layered joke about Homer's music knowledge and Yoko Ono criticism

jokes.text: Less yappin', more zappin'.
jokes.setup: Doctor warns about experimental laser eye surgery risks
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses safety concerns with rhyming demand for action
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical Homer line with good rhythm and character consistency

jokes.text: Blair Witch repellent, antler saw and deep-woods Scrabble
jokes.setup: Marge lists what they bought for Bart's camping trip
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and specific camping supplies
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity with topical Blair Witch reference

jokes.text: Wait. You gotta use these drops. They prevent your eyes from crusting over. / Oh, here we go with the add-ons. No, thanks.
jokes.setup: Doctor recommends necessary eye drops after surgery
jokes.punchline: Homer treats medical necessity like car dealership upselling
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation about consumer suspicion applied inappropriately to healthcare

jokes.text: Oh, he'll never dance with her. She'll have to settle for some Mexican Milhouse.
jokes.setup: Lisa watches tango movie where hero must choose dance partner
jokes.punchline: Lisa's oddly specific prediction about settling for lesser option
jokes.explanation: Funny cultural observation with Lisa's intellectual way of describing romantic comedy tropes

jokes.text: My name is... Lisabella. / [Gasps] That's my name with 'bella' on the end of it.
jokes.setup: Romantic lead asks heroine her name in tango movie
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately recognizes the obvious name derivation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on lazy romantic movie naming conventions

jokes.text: Qué malo. Once again, I must sugar my own churro.
jokes.setup: Rejected suitor in tango movie watches hero choose another partner
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic Spanish euphemism for being alone/rejected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sexual innuendo disguised as food metaphor, very quotable

jokes.text: Only one man was crazy enough to dance that dance, and he is dead. / My twin brother, Freduardo. / But where he died, I shall live- in his apartment.
jokes.setup: Dramatic buildup about dangerous dance and dead brother
jokes.punchline: Anticlimatic revelation he just wants the apartment
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from tragic romanticism to mundane real estate concerns

jokes.text: You are now carrying my child. / But how? / It is the mystery of the dance.
jokes.setup: Woman discovers pregnancy after dancing
jokes.punchline: Vague mystical explanation for biological impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good parody of overwrought romance movie mysticism and bad plotting

jokes.text: [Imitating Marge] Now, Homie, when we get to the liquor store... buy me some Jack Daniels and a carton of smokes. / Yes, dear. [Both Snickering]
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart mock Marge while she's away
jokes.punchline: Homer's exaggerated impression makes Marge sound like an alcoholic smoker
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Homer and Bart bonding through mild mockery

jokes.text: Well, I think World War II helped a little, Mom. / Don't smart-mouth, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Marge claims Vicki Valentine's dancing ended the Depression
jokes.punchline: Lisa's factual correction gets shut down as smart-mouthing
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics with Lisa's intellectual nature conflicting with family mythos

jokes.text: I'm just plain Vicki now. / All right. I'd like to sign my daughter up for lessons, Vicki. / Little Vicki. / But you just said- / So what dance style were you interested in?
jokes.setup: Vicki says she's just plain Vicki now, not Little Vicki
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects Marge to use Little Vicki then changes subject
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing aging performer clinging to past identity

jokes.text: [Cracks] Ow! You stepped on my toes again! / If you would just let me lead- / You're not a leader, Seymour. Never have been, never will be. / Oh!
jokes.setup: Ballroom dancing demonstration going poorly
jokes.punchline: Woman's devastating personal attack on partner's leadership abilities
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with relationship dynamics spilling into dance lesson

jokes.text: [Rhythmically] Oh, the birdie in the cage out, lady in the center. Now the birdie is purty and the crows hop in. Put the feather all together, hell-bent for leather. Put your honey in the saddle-
jokes.setup: Demonstration of square dancing calls
jokes.punchline: Increasingly nonsensical and sexually suggestive square dance terminology
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of square dance jargon but goes on too long without strong payoff

jokes.text: Little advice, don't live through your child. / Okay.
jokes.setup: Marge pushes for what Lisa wants vs. what Vicki offers
jokes.punchline: Former child star tells parent not to live through child; Marge immediately agrees
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony with former child performer giving parenting advice, Marge's quick capitulation

jokes.text: This is gonna be the best week of our young lives. / I'm gonna pound you two all week. / Oh. What'd we do? / Nothin'. But I gotta pound somebody.
jokes.setup: Kids excited about camp before meeting counselor
jokes.punchline: Bully's honest admission he needs to beat someone up regardless of reason
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about arbitrary childhood violence and bully psychology

jokes.text: I don't wanna go home. My grandma's sleeping in my bed, and she has skin like a basketball.
jokes.setup: Kids decide not to go home when camp falls through
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's disturbing but specific description of grandmother's skin texture
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse character moment with uncomfortable but vivid imagery

jokes.text: Yeah. It's just like my dad always says: For an evening or a week, there's no place like the mall. Food, fun and fashion- the mall has it all. / What? What did he say?
jokes.setup: Bart suggests staying at the mall instead of going home
jokes.punchline: Recites obviously made-up inspirational mall motto as if it's Homer's wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Bart's manipulation skills and Homer's alleged mall obsession

jokes.text: Teacher, my shoes are making noise. / You must be Ralph. / My daddy shoots people. [Grunts]
jokes.setup: Ralph complains about tap shoes making noise during tap class
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about his police chief father shooting people
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph Wiggum non-sequitur with dark undertones about police violence

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Somebody's off the beat. Let me hear you two. [tapping] Okay, now you. [Lisa taps badly] Whoa. [Shouts] / [All Laughing] / Children, stop it. / For all you know, she has a medical condition. / Nope. / I see. [Groans]
jokes.setup: Little Vicki identifies someone dancing off-beat in class
jokes.punchline: Lisa's terrible dancing causes laughter, medical excuse fails
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Lisa's dancing struggles with teacher trying to be diplomatic

jokes.text: Where are we goin'? / Quiet. Just keep scurrying. [Screaming, Grunting] / Worms! / Gold! / Wait, this is just chocolate. Chocolate! / And these are gummy worms. Gummy! / Warheads. Jelly Bellies. We're like two kids in a candy store. / [Together] Yes!
jokes.setup: Boys discover candy store in mall and get excited about treats
jokes.punchline: Bart's literal 'kids in a candy store' observation with enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Obvious pun payoff but works for the characters' childlike excitement

jokes.text: Whoa! We're lumberjacks!
jokes.setup: Boys playing in mall fountain
jokes.punchline: Bart's random declaration while apparently wielding mall props
jokes.explanation: Random child logic humor. Works because it's authentically how kids play-act with available props.

jokes.text: There's candy chewed, wigs pulled from stands... cheese packages poked and repoked. / Every sign points to one obvious culprit- a giant rat.
jokes.setup: Health inspector catalogs mall damage caused by Bart and Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Concludes obviously human vandalism must be from giant rat
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and authority figure incompetence trope

jokes.text: Well, you're falling a lot. Maybe you should work on that.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what she's doing wrong in dance class
jokes.punchline: Vicki's bluntly obvious observation about Lisa's constant falling
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of painfully obvious advice

jokes.text: Why, back when I was your age, I had 43 movies under my belt... and I had to do it without tappa-tappa-tappa. I would have killed for tappa-tappa-tappa.
jokes.setup: Lisa frustrated with basic instruction, Vicki defends her teaching
jokes.punchline: Vicki's obsessive repetition of 'tappa-tappa-tappa' as if it's profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing aging performer's limited teaching ability

jokes.text: Well, you'll never save Grandpa's farm with that attitude. / You've just got to turn that frown upside down. / That's a smile, not an upside-down frown. / Work on that too.
jokes.setup: Vicki gives Lisa generic inspirational advice about attitude
jokes.punchline: Corrects Lisa's smile as being wrong kind of facial expression
jokes.explanation: Good deconstruction of empty motivational speak and facial expression logic

jokes.text: Why does it have a picture of Vitamin Barn? / Didn't you ever go to camp? / The old Vitamin Barn.
jokes.setup: Postcard from Bart shows Vitamin Barn instead of camp
jokes.punchline: Homer's fond memories of corporate vitamin store as camp experience
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment with misplaced nostalgia for commercial establishment

jokes.text: Look at you, all sugar and spice instead of equations and test tubes. / You're Daddy's precious dancing queen.
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to quit dancing but parents interrupt with pride
jokes.punchline: Homer's stereotypical gender expectations about daughters
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's limited understanding of Lisa's interests and gender stereotyping

jokes.text: My kitty cat's sick... and I'd be ever so sad if she should die. [Coughing] / Well, I'm no animal doctor or nothin'... but whenever I'm feelin' poorly, you know what fixes me up? / Dancin'? / Dancin'.
jokes.setup: Vicki's sick cat story, Carl offers help despite not being veterinarian
jokes.punchline: Carl's simple solution of dancing for medical problems
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with dancing as universal cure-all theme

jokes.text: [Sighs] The cat dances better than I do.
jokes.setup: Lisa watches Carl teach dancing to Vicki's sick cat
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dejected comparison of her skills to a sick cat
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment showing her perfectionist frustration

jokes.text: Gosh, that cheese looks good. Think I could grab it before that anvil hits? / I don't know, Chief. It's a million to one. / I like those odds.
jokes.setup: Wiggum spots cheese in obvious cartoon trap with anvil
jokes.punchline: Despite terrible odds, Wiggum likes the challenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chief Wiggum character moment with his backwards logic and overconfidence

jokes.text: My mistake was grabbing the cheese. [Coughs]
jokes.setup: Wiggum gets hit by anvil after grabbing cheese from trap
jokes.punchline: His analysis completely misses the obvious trap setup
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to cheese trap with Wiggum's predictably wrong lesson learned

jokes.text: Professor Frink, will that spaceship be ready for the recital? / I have visited the future, and yes, it will. [Gibberish]
jokes.setup: Vicki asks Frink about spaceship prop for dance recital
jokes.punchline: Frink claims time travel knowledge for simple prop question
jokes.explanation: Good Professor Frink character moment with over-complicated answer to simple question

jokes.text: Excuse me. Why isn't my name in the program? / It is, silly. You've got the most important part of all. / 'Curtain puller'? / No one can see the show if the curtain isn't open.
jokes.setup: Lisa discovers she's not listed as a performer in the recital program
jokes.punchline: Vicki spins curtain-pulling duty as the most important role
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with transparent attempt to make Lisa feel better about demotion

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Lisa, but giving everyone an equal part... when they're clearly not equal is called what again, class? / Communism! / That's right.
jokes.setup: Lisa wants equal participation in dance recital
jokes.punchline: Vicki frames fairness as communist indoctrination
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about Cold War paranoia applied to children's activities

jokes.text: And I didn't tap all those Morse code messages to the Allies... till my shoes filled with blood... just to roll out the welcome mat for the Reds.
jokes.setup: Vicki continues her anti-communist rant about equal participation
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous backstory about tap dancing as wartime espionage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Vicki's character with absurd wartime dancing heroics

jokes.text: 'Scuse me, Lisa, but I couldn't help but overhear your nerdly predicament. / Maybe I can be of assistance with the dancing and the twisting... and the kung fu fighting. [Gibberish]
jokes.setup: Professor Frink offers to help Lisa with her dancing problems
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly ridiculous dance styles including kung fu fighting
jokes.explanation: Standard Professor Frink character moment with his verbose, technical approach to simple problems

jokes.text: It's most entertaining, but how does that help me? / Observe. [Muttering] / Ooh! That's brilliant, Professor. What will you think of next? / Well, I also found this at the gift shop. Isn't it cute? / I'm hoping to turn it into a weapon. [Cackling Gibberish] It'll kill ya.
jokes.setup: Frink shows Lisa dancing robot solution, she asks what he'll invent next
jokes.punchline: Innocent gift shop item that Frink wants to weaponize
jokes.explanation: Good Frink character moment showing his tendency toward dangerous inventions

jokes.text: Are you saying he's right on the other side of that plant? [Whining] / [Snarls] / Okay. Run!
jokes.setup: Wiggum and Lou looking for escaped lion, Lou makes whining sounds
jokes.punchline: Lion is literally right there, causing immediate panic
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick with predictable lion chase setup

jokes.text: Bart, you're a genius. [Screams]
jokes.setup: Milhouse praises Bart's plan right before something goes wrong
jokes.punchline: Immediate scream suggesting plan has backfired
jokes.explanation: Predictable timing gag with praise immediately followed by disaster

jokes.text: Well, well. Looks like the cat got the rat. / And that's the end of that tale.
jokes.setup: Lion catches the boys who were mistaken for giant rat
jokes.punchline: Security guard's satisfied wordplay about cat and rat plus 'tail' pun
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that resolves the giant rat subplot but lacks wit

jokes.text: Now this may hurt a lot. What am I saying, 'may'? [Both Gasp] / Ow! / Now that's a happy face.
jokes.setup: Vicki prepares to create artificial dimples for recital
jokes.punchline: Corrects herself to guarantee pain, then satisfied with painful result
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about children's entertainment industry's harsh realities

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Lisa. People go to a children's dance recital expecting a certain level of professionalism.
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to participate in recital
jokes.punchline: Little Vicki's pompous attitude about children's dance recital standards
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Little Vicki takes children's recital as seriously as Broadway. The pretension is the joke.

jokes.text: [Groaning] I ate too much plastic candy. / Heavens to betsy. The star of the show is sick. / Whatever will we do? There's only one person who can get us out of this pickle.
jokes.setup: Ralph gets sick from fake candy, creating crisis for show
jokes.punchline: Vicki's dramatic setup suggesting Lisa will save the day
jokes.explanation: Standard plot setup with predictable misdirection about who will help

jokes.text: Lisa? / Yes? / Help me into Ralph's costume. [Sighs]
jokes.setup: Lisa thinks she's finally getting her chance to perform
jokes.punchline: Vicki just wants Lisa to help her get into costume instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that maintains Vicki's selfish character while disappointing Lisa

jokes.text: Oh, it's too important. I'll do it for you. [Sighs]
jokes.setup: Vicki gives Lisa one job as curtain puller
jokes.punchline: Takes even that simple task away from Lisa
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to curtain puller setup, showing Vicki's complete control issues

jokes.text: Where's Lisa? / Shh! This plot is hard enough to follow as it is.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for Lisa during confusing dance recital
jokes.punchline: Marge's meta-commentary about the convoluted storyline
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke acknowledging the episode's complex plotting

jokes.text: Where's Lisa? / Shh! This plot is hard enough to follow as it is.
jokes.setup: Homer looks for Lisa during recital
jokes.punchline: Marge's admission that the children's show plot is confusing
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor acknowledging the absurdity of the recital's space theme. Marge's concentration is funny.

jokes.text: Wow. Look at her go. / Yeah, that pressure we put on her really paid off.
jokes.setup: Parents watch Lisa finally dancing well in the recital
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud admission that parental pressure was the key
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor about parenting methods and taking credit for children's success

jokes.text: Nobody upstages Little Vicki. [Hisses]
jokes.setup: Lisa's electronic shoes make her dance incredibly well, overshadowing Vicki
jokes.punchline: Vicki's villainous reaction to being outperformed by student
jokes.explanation: Good character escalation showing Vicki's true competitive nature

jokes.text: Stop the clapping! You'll kill us all!
jokes.setup: Lisa's shoes responding to applause by dancing more violently
jokes.punchline: Vicki's dramatic proclamation about lethal applause
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd situation with dramatic life-or-death stakes

jokes.text: I once destroyed Buddy Ebsen's credit rating. / Why? / He knows why.
jokes.setup: Vicki admits to doing crazy things when desperate
jokes.punchline: Ominous reference to attacking obscure celebrity's finances
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random and dark character detail with mysterious grudge against Buddy Ebsen

jokes.text: They're clearly in the on position. See? 'On.' / I was merely trying to spare the girl's feelings, you insensitive clod.
jokes.setup: Homer corrects Frink about shoe switch position
jokes.punchline: Frink admits he was lying to protect Lisa's feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frink's kindness beneath his scientific demeanor. Homer's obliviousness adds to it.

jokes.text: Jesus, Mary and glavin! These shoes are in the off position. / You mean I danced all by myself? / See, honey? All you needed was to believe- / What are you talking about, Professor Frink? They're clearly in the on position. See? 'On.'
jokes.setup: Frink claims shoes were off, suggesting Lisa danced naturally
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately contradicts him, shoes were actually on
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that builds up Lisa's confidence before deflating it

jokes.text: I guess I'm never gonna be a Broadway baby. / That's not true, honey. You can always write a depressing Broadway play of some kind. / You think so? / Sure. It could be a story about people coming to terms with things. / Hey, yeah. You could load it up with lots of swears. That's what David Mamet does.
jokes.setup: Lisa disappointed about not being a dancer
jokes.punchline: Parents' encouragement about writing depressing plays with profanity
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference with Homer's surprisingly specific knowledge of David Mamet

jokes.text: Hey, yeah. You could load it up with lots of swears. That's what David Mamet does.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lisa writing Broadway plays
jokes.punchline: Bart's sophisticated knowledge of David Mamet's style
jokes.explanation: Great Bart moment - his unexpected cultural knowledge paired with focus on profanity. Perfect character blend of sophistication and juvenile interests.

jokes.text: Why, it's just a cute little weasel. Hello, there, Mr. Weasel. / Isn't that cute? [Screaming] / [Electricity Crackling] / I'm down!
jokes.setup: Marge sees what she thinks is a harmless weasel
jokes.punchline: Turns out to be dangerous, causes electrical accident
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection gag with predictable dangerous animal attack
